cards.back: The process of visualizing the structure of a neural network by removing layers one by one, starting from the input layer, to understand how each layer processes information
A type of neural network that uses feedback connections to process sequential data, allowing it to maintain internal state and capture long-term dependencies
A type of RNN that processes input data in both forward and backward directions, allowing it to capture both past and future contexts in sequential data
A type of RNN that has multiple layers, allowing it to learn long-term dependencies and relationships in sequential data
A type of neural network that uses recursive functions to process sequential data, allowing it to capture long-term dependencies and relationships
A type of RNN that uses memory cells and gates to learn long-term dependencies in sequential data, overcoming the vanishing gradient problem
